title: SEC0: onerror never resolves open_rx — connect hangs forever on WS failure
description: File: wasm/src/transport.rs, lines 123-127 and 43-55. The onerror closure does not hold a clone of open_tx_cell and never sends Err(...) into the open oneshot. When a WebSocket connection fails (bad URL, TLS error, server down), the browser fires onerror followed by onclose. The onclose closure (lines 131-138) only drains the pending in-flight request map — it has no access to open_tx_cell either. Result: open_rx.await in client.rs line 29-31 blocks forever. NieRelayClient::connect() hangs with no timeout, no error, no cancellation path. Correct behavior: the onerror closure must receive a clone of open_tx_cell and send Err(String) into it when the error fires. The onclose closure should also send Err('disconnected') if the oneshot has not yet been resolved.
